name: shopify_store_scraper
title: Shopify Store Scraper
description: Fetch a public Shopify collection page and normalize product titles, handles, URLs, prices, availability, and images without relying on the commonly blocked products.json feed.

seo.title: Shopify Store Scraper Tool for Structured Web Data
seo.description: Fetch a public Shopify collection page and normalize product titles, handles, URLs, prices, availability, and images. Better Fetch returns clean structured records ready for agents, exports, monitoring, and downstream workflows.
seo.intro: Fetch a public Shopify collection page and normalize product titles, handles, URLs, prices, availability, and images. The tool deliberately reads storefront HTML instead of the frequently rate-limited products.json endpoint, keeping ordinary runs on Better Fetch's low-cost one-credit path. Results can be inspected in the web workspace, exported as JSON or CSV, called through REST, or composed by an AI agent over MCP.

useCases.title: Agent research
useCases.description: Give AI agents bounded, normalized shopify store scraper results without writing source-specific retrieval and parsing code.

useCases.title: Repeatable monitoring
useCases.description: Save the configuration, rerun it on a consistent source, and export comparable structured artifacts for operational review.

faqs.question: What does Shopify Store Scraper return?
faqs.answer: It returns normalized structured fields documented in the output schema, plus the fetched source URL and a record count for receipts and exports.

faqs.question: Does this tool store the retrieved output?
faqs.answer: No. Better Fetch records privacy-safe run metadata for reliability and retention analysis, but the output payload itself is not stored by the workspace.
